summary_text:
S:
The patient reports right elbow pain following a box-lifting incident yesterday. He describes a popping sensation and strain while lifting. He reports ice provides some relief, but ibuprofen provides only temporary relief, with throbbing returning upon ice removal. He denies any other symptoms. He reports a history of anaphylaxis and a previous carotid surgery.

O:
Physical exam reveals tenderness over the lateral epicondyle, swelling, and redness of the right elbow. Range of motion is limited with flexion and extension. Dorsal forearm pain is noted. X-ray shows normal bony alignment with mild swelling at the lateral epicondyle. No fracture is identified.

A:
The patient's symptoms are consistent with lateral epicondylitis. The history of anaphylaxis is noted for future management.

P:
Rescribe ibuprofen 800 mg every six hours as needed. Provide a sling for comfort. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one week if symptoms do not improve. Order medications and procedures.
reference_summary:
CC:

Right elbow pain.

HPI:

Ms. Hernandez is a 48-year-old female who presents today for an evaluation of right elbow pain. She states she was moving boxes from the truck into the house yesterday and felt a pop in her elbow. She has tried applying ice and taking Ibuprofen, but it does not help. She has a history of anaphylaxis and has an EpiPen. Her past surgical history is significant for neck surgery.

EXAM

Examination of the right elbow shows tenderness over the lateral epicondyle. Swelling and redness are noted. Pain with flexion and extension of the elbow. Pain over the dorsal aspect of the forearm.

RESULTS

X-rays of the right elbow shows no obvious signs of acute fracture. Mild effusion about the lateral aspect.

IMPRESSION

Right elbow lateral epicondylitis.

PLAN

At this point, I discussed the diagnosis and treatment options with the patient. I have recommended a sling for comfort. We discussed ice and anti-inflammatory medications. I will prescribe Motrin, 800 mg to take every 6 hours. She will follow up with me as needed if she continues to have pain. All questions were answered.

section_miscategorizations.text: The history of anaphylaxis is noted for future management.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a past medical history element already reported by the patient in S; in A it is framed only as a note for future consideration, not as an active assessment or diagnostic impression.

The content is appropriate for the Plan section but is overly vague and duplicative; it does not clearly specify distinct orders beyond what is already listed. This is an organizational issue rather than a true miscategorization.
